findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Blocker
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch. This is critical as it impacts the lawful basis for processing behavioral data and consent mechanisms for cross-device tracking.
assumptions: GDPR compliance is mandatory for EU market launch.

evidence.excerpt: Legal team has flagged that the PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch.

recommendations: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit immediately.

findings.title: User Behavior Tracking Pipeline Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of the ML recommendation engine is dependent on the completion of the user behavior tracking pipeline, which must handle at least 50k events/second.
assumptions: The data ingestion pipeline can be built to handle the required throughput.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics for each authenticated user.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of the user behavior tracking pipeline.

findings.title: Phased Delivery Plan
findings.description: Propose a phased delivery plan: MVP should include the user behavior tracking pipeline and GDPR compliance audit. V1 should focus on the ML recommendation engine and user preference dashboard. V2 should add cross-device tracking and data export for advertisers.
assumptions: Phased delivery will allow for iterative feedback and compliance checks.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history...

recommendations: Implement the phased delivery plan as outlined.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Tracking
findings.description: Privacy risks have been flagged due to the nature of data collection and potential for re-identification of users from anonymized data. This could lead to legal challenges.
assumptions: Anonymization techniques must be robust to prevent re-identification.

evidence.excerpt: The anonymization pipeline needs validation — legal has flagged that with enough cohort intersections, individual re-identification may be possible.

recommendations: Enhance anonymization techniques and validate with legal.

findings.title: Cross-Device Tracking Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of cross-device tracking is dependent on the completion of the user behavior tracking pipeline and the creation of a cross-device identity graph.
assumptions: Cross-device identity graph can be built effectively.

evidence.excerpt: Link user activity across mobile app and web browser sessions using deterministic matching.

recommendations: Ensure the user behavior tracking pipeline is completed before starting cross-device tracking.

summary: The analysis identifies critical risks related to GDPR compliance and privacy, which are blockers for the project. Dependencies on the user behavior tracking pipeline are crucial for the recommendation engine and cross-device tracking. A phased delivery plan is recommended to manage complexity and ensure compliance.
